basis: Anchor 30 plan lists Urza as a mono-U artifacts/control Commander anchor.
EDHREC, cEDH pages, Moxfield/Archidekt cEDH lists and Commander articles label Urza as Commander/cEDH context.
cEDH stax/combo and casual artifact value are kept as separate power lanes.

source_limit_notes: Aggregate/manual reference only; no copied public decklist.
Hard stax and expensive fast mana are not casual defaults.
Compact artifact combo/control packages are strongest in high-power/cEDH lanes.

themes.name: artifacts_control
themes.notes: Urza turns artifacts into mana and breaks parity on artifact-heavy engines.

themes.name: mono_blue_combo_stax
themes.notes: Stax and compact infinite mana packages are proven cEDH patterns but must be power-gated.

themes.name: construct_token_value
themes.notes: Artifact density also grows Urza's Construct and supports fair artifact value plans.

examples: Winter Orb lock without high-power intent
avoid_patterns.reason: Urza stax is proven but socially risky outside explicit high-power/cEDH requests.

examples: random artifacts without mana, control, or combo role
avoid_patterns.reason: Urza wants artifacts that become mana, card advantage, stax, or combo pieces.

examples: reserved-list or very expensive fast mana as mandatory
avoid_patterns.reason: High-budget cards should be optional signals, not product defaults.
